Ferulago is a genus of flowering plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae), characterized by its umbels of small white or purple flowers and slender stems. The health benefits of Ferulago include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Ferulago include ferulic acid, a polyphenol with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Ferulago include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
